resources.string: to tap before the screen locks
A button appears that you tap to confirm you are awake. If you don\'t, the screen locks.
Playback simply stops after the interval, also with the screen off (music, audiobooks, podcasts).
No browser found to open the link.
Overlay permission required. Please grant it in Settings.
Device Admin permission required to lock the screen.
Please disable battery optimization for SleepGuard so the button reliably appears in the background.
SleepGuard needs this permission solely to lock the screen (like pressing the power button) when you fall asleep.

SleepGuard locks your phone when you fall asleep watching videos — so the battery isn\'t empty by morning and you never lose your place in a film.\n\nSleepGuard will keep showing you a button that you have to tap. If you don\'t tap it, the phone gets locked. You set the interval between the buttons in minutes — and how long you have to tap, in seconds.
Allow notifications so you can always see that SleepGuard is active while it runs in the background.
SleepGuard shows a small button on top of your video. For that it needs permission to display over other apps.
To turn the screen off when you fall asleep — just like the power button — SleepGuard needs device administrator permission. It is used only to lock the screen.
So the check button still appears on time while another app is in the foreground, please allow SleepGuard to ignore battery optimization.
You\'re ready. Set your interval, tap Start, and keep watching.
